headline: Russia's Turla unleashed a fresh STOCKSTAY backdoor on Ukraine as CISA logged the first in-the-wild exploitation of a PTC Windchill flaw and phishing kits sharpened their evasion of multifactor authentication.

emerging_trends.text: Russia's Turla and an unattributed crew wielding the SharkLoader dropper both bored into government networks this week, a sign that state-grade espionage tooling keeps multiplying across new actors.
Defenders raced patches as CISA recorded the first in-the-wild exploitation of a PTC Windchill flaw and attackers weaponized a Cisco Unified CM bug within a day.
emerging_trends.topic_title: CISA Flags Windchill and Cisco Exploitation

emerging_trends.text: Phishing kits like Mirage2FA and Bluekit folded HTML smuggling and browser-in-the-middle tricks into their playbooks, slipping past multifactor authentication on Microsoft 365.

topics.title: CISA Flags Windchill and Cisco Exploitation
topics.latest_developments: CISA added PTC Windchill and FlexPLM remote-code-execution flaw CVE-2026-12569, its first observed in-the-wild exploitation, plus Cisco Unified Communications Manager server-side request forgery flaw CVE-2026-20230 to the Known Exploited Vulnerabilities catalog, while attackers weaponized the Cisco bug within 24 hours and a Lantronix serial-to-IP converter flaw, CVE-2025-67038, came under attack.
topics.summary: The Cisco CUCM flaw grants request forgery and root escalation; administrators must patch Windchill, Cisco Unified CM, and Lantronix devices now.

sources.title: First-Ever Exploitation of PTC Windchill Vulnerability Discovered in the Wild
CISA Adds Two Known Exploited Vulnerabilities to Catalog
In Less Than 24 Hours, Attackers Weaponize Cisco CUCM Flaw
Lantronix Serial-to-IP Converter Flaw Exploited in Attacks After OT Threat Warning

topics.title: Phishing Kits Sharpen MFA Evasion
topics.latest_developments: Fortra identified Mirage2FA, a kit pairing short-lived HTML smuggling with obfuscated JavaScript to steal Microsoft 365 logins during MFA prompts; the Bluekit phishing-as-a-service platform added browser-in-the-middle theft and nearly 70 new hostnames in a week; and criminals abused Shopify's Shop order-tracking app, planting fake receipts to trigger callback phishing.
topics.summary: Phishing-as-a-service kits increasingly defeat multifactor authentication and ride trusted apps; train users and tighten Microsoft 365 sign-in monitoring.

sources.title: Mirage2FA phishing kit uses HTML smuggling to steal Microsoft 365 credentials
Bluekit phishing kit adopts browser-in-the-middle for login theft
Order-tracking app Shop abused to push callback phishing attacks

topics.title: Third-Party Suppliers Open Europe to Ransomware
topics.latest_developments: Black Kite's 2026 European Cyber Risk Report, drawn from 2,066 ransomware incidents across 31 countries between January 2025 and April 2026, found attacks accelerating with third-party suppliers as a leading entry point, the same day a third-party vendor compromise let hackers steal roughly $3 million from Polymarket users and Dark Reading reported edtech attackers pivoting from schools to their software vendors.
topics.summary: Outside vendors increasingly open the door to ransomware and theft; map supplier access and monitor every third-party connection.

sources.title: Ransomware gangs find Europe’s weakest link in third-party suppliers
$3 Million Reportedly Stolen in Polymarket Hack
EdTech Attackers Shift From Schools to Their Software Suppliers

topics.title: Turla Deploys STOCKSTAY Against Ukraine
topics.latest_developments: Google's Threat Intelligence Group attributed a previously undocumented .NET backdoor, STOCKSTAY, to Turla, which deployed it against Ukrainian government and military organizations and entities interested in Italian foreign policy.
topics.summary: Turla, the Russian FSB-linked espionage group, continually rebuilds the Windows backdoor to steal data from state and defense networks.

sources.title: Russian APT Deploys ‘StockStay’ Backdoor Against Ukrainian Targets
Google Details Turla's New STOCKSTAY Backdoor Used in Ukraine Espionage Attacks

topics.title: SharkLoader Dropper Hits Governments and Developers
topics.latest_developments: Kaspersky uncovered StrikeShark, a global campaign wielding the previously unknown SharkLoader dropper to compromise government organizations and software development companies across multiple countries, first spotted in an attack on a diplomatic organization in Indonesia.
topics.summary: The unattributed operators and novel dropper mark a fresh espionage actor reaching public-sector and developer targets worldwide.

sources.title: Mystery hackers use novel SharkLoader dropper against governments, software devs

topics.title: Malware Targets AI Analysis Tools
topics.latest_developments: Researchers detailed Gaslight, macOS malware that buries prompt-injection strings and fake debugging data in its executable to mislead AI-assisted analysis tools, while a new paper showed large language models fall for prompt injection because they learn the writing style of role blocks rather than their tags.
topics.summary: Attackers now aim at the AI tooling defenders rely on; treat model-driven triage output as untrusted and verify findings by hand.

sources.title: New macOS malware embeds fake errors to confuse AI analysis tools
Interesting Paper Exploring Prompt Injection

weather: Saturday: Showers And Thunderstorms Likely then Slight Chance Showers And Thunderstorms, high 80F.

games.recap: Brandon Lowe, Henry Davis each homer to lead Pirates over Mariners 5-1

business_politics.title: Global AI Selloff Triggers Korean Trading Halts
business_politics.latest_developments: South Korea's market slid more than 8% on June 26, triggering its second circuit-breaker halt of the week, and a report that OpenAI may delay its public offering deepened a rout that earlier hit memory chips and Apple.
business_politics.summary: Investors dumping artificial-intelligence-linked shares sent the Kospi down over 8%, pressured Nasdaq futures and chipmakers worldwide, and pushed oil back toward pre-war levels, intensifying doubts about whether the AI boom can sustain its spending.

sources.title: South Korean Stocks Tumble, Triggering Second Trading Halt This Week
Nasdaq Futures Slip as Tech Selloff Deepens
Global markets fall as investors fret about AI demand

business_politics.title: JPMorgan Sets Dimon Succession Race
business_politics.latest_developments: JPMorgan named Doug Petno and Troy Rohrbaugh co-presidents on June 25, formally opening the contest to succeed 70-year-old chief executive Jamie Dimon.
business_politics.summary: JPMorgan Chase, the largest U.S. bank, elevated commercial-banking head Doug Petno and markets chief Troy Rohrbaugh to co-presidents, positioning the two as front-runners to eventually replace Jamie Dimon, who has run the firm since 2005.

sources.title: JPMorgan Shakes Up Dimon Succession Race With Two New Presidents

business.title: Engineer Charged Over Three Mile Island Restart Tip
business.latest_developments: A federal grand jury and the SEC allege a former nuclear engineer used secret information about Constellation Energy's Three Mile Island restart to profit roughly $1.48 million, TribLive reported June 26.
business.summary: The SEC says a Delaware nuclear engineer traded on nonpublic details of Constellation Energy's plan to reopen the Three Mile Island plant near Harrisburg, making about $1.48 million before the deal became public.

sources.title: Three Mile Island restart deal was secret. A nuclear engineer used info to profit $1.4M, grand jury said

business.title: Hoffmann Owners Pledge Penguins Stay in Pittsburgh
business.latest_developments: The Hoffmann Family of Companies introduced itself at PPG Paints Arena on June 26, vowing to keep the Penguins in Pittsburgh, after the NHL approved the sale earlier this week.
business.summary: Pittsburgh's new Penguins owners, the Hoffmann Family of Companies, told a PPG Paints Arena press conference they bought a Squirrel Hill home, have no plans to move the team, and floated changing the franchise's ECHL affiliation, with Kyle Dubas staying on.

sources.title: Penguins' new owners talk keeping team in Pittsburgh, possible ECHL change and more

business.title: Schwebel's Closure Hits Bakery Communities
business.latest_developments: The Post-Gazette detailed June 26 the community loss as Schwebel Baking Company winds down, following the liquidation and outlet-store closures already reported.
business.summary: Schwebel Baking Company's wind-down after 120 years strands western Pennsylvania workers and longtime customers, the Post-Gazette reported, describing 'a heavy impact' across the region's neighborhoods.

sources.title: 'A heavy impact:' Schwebel's closure leaves a sense of community loss

around_town.title: PennDOT Tests Commercial Street Bridge Move
around_town.latest_developments: PennDOT crews closed Commercial Street and the Nine Mile Run trail on June 26 to test the equipment that will slide a 22-million-pound replacement span into place next month, the step previewed earlier this week.
around_town.summary: PennDOT ran a full equipment test under Interstate 376 near Frick Park for the Commercial Street bridge replacement, ahead of a planned I-376 closure next month to move the new 22-million-pound span.

sources.title: PennDOT performs major test for upcoming Commercial Street bridge replacement project. Here's what to know.

around_town.title: County Weighs 18-Week Paid Parental Leave
around_town.latest_developments: An Allegheny County Council committee heard public testimony June 25 on a proposal granting county employees 18 weeks of paid parental leave.
around_town.summary: Allegheny County Council's committee took public comment on an 18-week paid-parental-leave plan for county workers, with UPMC Magee-Womens Hospital nurse Jean Stone among those urging a stronger policy across the Pittsburgh region.

sources.title: Allegheny County Council committee hears from the public about 18-week paid parental leave proposal

around_town.title: Woodland Hills Superintendent Testifies at Hearing
around_town.latest_developments: Suspended Woodland Hills superintendent Joe Maluchnik testified June 25 on the fifth night of his public firing hearing, telling his side for the first time.
around_town.summary: Joe Maluchnik, the suspended Woodland Hills School District superintendent, told the public hearing over his firing that the district resisted his efforts to improve it, his first account of the months-long dispute.

sources.title: Suspended Woodland Hills superintendent testifies on night 5 of public hearing

around_teams.title: Why NFL Kickers, Boswell, Boot Farther
around_teams.latest_developments: A Post-Gazette feature June 26 examined why Steelers kicker Chris Boswell and NFL kickers leaguewide are making field goals from record distances.
around_teams.summary: The Post-Gazette traced the equipment, technique, and rule changes letting kickers like the Steelers' Chris Boswell set distance records, part of an NFL-wide surge in long field goals.

sources.title: Why are Chris Boswell and other NFL kickers booting the football farther than ever before?

around_teams.title: Ozuna May Be Turning the Corner
around_teams.latest_developments: A Post-Gazette analysis June 25 weighed whether Marcell Ozuna is finally heating up after a lousy start with the Pirates.
around_teams.summary: Pirates designated hitter Marcell Ozuna, brought in to add power, has shown recent signs of life at the plate, the Post-Gazette reported alongside notes on manager Don Kelly, Ryan O'Hearn, and Oneil Cruz.

sources.title: Marcell Ozuna got off to a lousy start with the Pirates. Might he finally be turning the corner?

around_teams.title: Ebron Joins Not Just Football
around_teams.latest_developments: Eric Ebron appeared on Cam Heyward's 'Not Just Football' podcast, posted June 24, discussing Andrew Luck's retirement and the 11-0 Steelers team.
around_teams.summary: On Cam Heyward's 'Not Just Football,' former tight end Eric Ebron shared locker-room stories from Detroit, Indianapolis, and Pittsburgh, reflected on his own retirement at 28, and touched on Bill Belichick taking over North Carolina.

sources.source: Not Just Football with Cam Heyward
sources.title: Eric Ebron Talks Andrew Luck's Retirement, The 11-0 Steelers Team, & More! | Not Just Football

team_usa.title: USMNT Loses to Türkiye, Draws Bosnia
team_usa.latest_developments: Türkiye beat the United States 3-2 on Kaan Ayhan's final-kick goal Thursday, June 25, and the U.S., already Group D winners, drew Bosnia and Herzegovina in the round of 32 at Santa Clara on Wednesday, July 1.
team_usa.summary: Mauricio Pochettino's heavily rotated United States side conceded three goals at SoFi Stadium in Inglewood, with backup keeper Matt Turner faulted, in a meaningless group finale that still left the co-hosts atop Group D and bound for a knockout match against Bosnia and Herzegovina.

sources.title: USMNT to face Bosnia in World Cup round of 32
USA suffer late defeat to Turkey but eye Bosnia and Herzegovina in World Cup knockout stage

team_usa.title: Pulisic Set to Start Knockout Match
team_usa.latest_developments: Christian Pulisic came off the bench against Türkiye and looks ready to start the United States' round-of-32 game versus Bosnia and Herzegovina, ESPN reported June 26.
team_usa.summary: Christian Pulisic, recovered from the calf injury that limited him in the group stage, substituted on against Türkiye and is positioned to lead the U.S. attack in its first knockout match.

sources.title: Pulisic looks ready to make his mark on USMNT's World Cup

reading.author: Stratechery
reading.title: An Interview with Figma CEO Dylan Field About Design and AI
reading.summary: Ben Thompson interviews Figma chief executive Dylan Field on building the design platform and why he believes AI gives the company a tailwind.

reading.author: Ed Zitron
reading.title: Cargo Culture
reading.summary: Zitron argues that much of the AI industry imitates the trappings of past tech successes while lacking the underlying substance, a cargo cult chasing the appearance of inevitability.

reading.author: Cal Newport
reading.title: Dear AI Companies: Stop the "Doom Trolling"
reading.summary: Newport calls on AI firms to quit stoking fears about their own products' dangers, likening it to a carmaker warning that its bestselling truck might be catastrophically unsafe.

sports_media: Not Just Football with Cam Heyward
